name: Lazarillo Trail (PR-TO 14)
name_original: Sendero del Lazarillo – circular desde Almorox a la peña escrita de Cenicientos (PR-TO 14)
description: Hike the moderate 10.0-mile Sendero del Lazarillo circular route from Almorox, exploring pine forests and historical sites with panoramic vi
region_intro: Hiking around Almorox features diverse natural landscapes, characterized by extensive pine forests and granitic rock formations. The region includes the Almorox Pine Forest, covering over 1,400 hectares. Waterways like the Alberche River wind through the territory, creating scenic environments. This varied terrain provides a range of hiking opportunities. Best hiking trails around Almorox The most popular hiking route is Lazarillo Trail (PR-TO 14), a 10.0 miles (16.0 km) trail that takes 4 hours 30 minutes to complete. This picturesque circular route traverses forests and meadows, featuring points of archaeological interest. Another top favourite among local hikers is Peña Cenicientos – Peña de Cenicientos loop from Cenicientos, a moderate 7.2 miles (11.7 km) path. This route offers views of granitic rock formations and the surrounding landscape. Local hikers also love the San Martín de Valdeiglesias loop from San Martín de Valdeiglesias, a 8.3 miles (13.4 km) trail leading through the Alberche River valley, often completed in about 4 hours. Hiking around Almorox is defined by expansive pine forests, granitic berrocales, and the serene Alberche River. The network offers options for different ability levels. The routes in Almorox are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.7 stars from over 80 reviews. More than 400 hikers have used komoot to explore Almorox's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Almorox?
A: Almorox offers a diverse network of over 35 hiking trails. These routes cater to various skill levels, with 9 easy, 25 moderate, and 2 difficult options, ensuring there's a suitable path for every hiker.

Q: What kind of natural landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Almorox?
A: Hiking in Almorox will immerse you in a rich tapestry of natural landscapes. You'll encounter the expansive &lt;strong&gt;Almorox Pine Forest&lt;/strong&gt;, characterized by its dense stone pines, and areas with striking granitic rock formations known as 'berrocales'. The &lt;strong&gt;Alberche River&lt;/strong&gt;, a tributary of the Tagus, also winds through the southern part of the municipality, offering serene poplar forests and picturesque riverine environments.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the Almorox area?
A: Yes, Almorox features several circular routes. A notable example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018390189/lazarillo-trail-pr-to-14" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lazarillo Trail (PR-TO 14)&lt;/a&gt;, which is a picturesque 16 km loop traversing forests and meadows, and includes points of archaeological interest. Another popular circular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10373173" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Cenicientos – Peña de Cenicientos loop from Cenicientos&lt;/a&gt;, offering scenic views of the granitic landscape.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or attractions I might encounter on the trails?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, hikers can discover historical and natural attractions. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018390189/lazarillo-trail-pr-to-14" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lazarillo Trail (PR-TO 14)&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, passes by an anthropomorphic tomb and the inscription of San Julián. You might also find historical structures like the Roman-era Bridge of the Barguillas and the 17th-century Bridge of Pasaderas over local streams. For natural points of interest, consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5662891" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Picadas Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2885390" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Juan Reservoir Marina&lt;/a&gt;, both accessible from nearby routes.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options suitable for children?
A: Almorox offers several easy and moderate trails that are suitable for families with children. The diverse network includes routes through the Almorox Pine Forest, which are generally well-maintained and offer a pleasant environment for a family outing. While specific 'child-friendly' routes aren't highlighted, the 9 easy trails available provide good starting points for families.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Almorox?
A: Many of the trails in Almorox are suitable for hiking with dogs, allowing you to explore the natural environment with your canine companion. The extensive pine forests and open meadows provide ample space for dogs to enjoy. However, it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near agricultural land, and to check local regulations for specific routes.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Almorox?
A: The best time to hike in Almorox is generally during spring and autumn when the temperatures are mild and the natural scenery is at its most vibrant. Spring brings blooming wildflowers, while autumn offers beautiful foliage. Summer can be quite warm, especially in the open areas, but the pine forests provide shade, and the Alberche River offers opportunities for cooling off. Winter hiking is also possible, with cooler temperatures and a different kind of serene beauty.

Q: Are there any trails that offer viewpoints or panoramic vistas?
A: While specific viewpoints are not explicitly listed for every trail, the region's granitic berrocales and its proximity to the Sierra de San Vicente and the Alberche valley suggest that many routes will offer elevated sections with scenic outlooks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10373173" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Cenicientos – Peña de Cenicientos loop from Cenicientos&lt;/a&gt;, for example, is likely to provide views of the surrounding landscape due to its name referencing 'Peña' (rocky peak).

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Almorox?
A: The hiking experience in Almorox is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.7 stars from over 80 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse terrain, from the expansive pine forests to the serene river valleys, and the variety of options available for different skill levels. The natural beauty and the opportunity to explore both natural and historical elements are frequently highlighted.

Q: Are there opportunities for wild swimming or cooling off near the hiking routes?
A: Yes, the &lt;strong&gt;Alberche River&lt;/strong&gt;, which flows through the southern part of Almorox, is a popular spot for bathing and picnics during the warmer months. Its clear waters and poplar-lined banks offer a refreshing break. Additionally, nearby reservoirs like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5662891" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Picadas Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2885390" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Juan Reservoir Marina&lt;/a&gt; also provide opportunities for water activities and cooling off after a hike.

Q: Is public transport available to access hiking trailheads in Almorox?
A: While Almorox is accessible by road, direct public transport options specifically to individual trailheads might be limited. It's advisable to check local bus schedules to Almorox town itself. For reaching specific starting points for hikes, a car is often the most convenient option, allowing greater flexibility to explore the wider network of trails.

Q: Are there parking facilities available near the hiking routes?
A: Yes, for many of the hiking routes in and around Almorox, you will find parking facilities, particularly near the town center or at designated starting points for popular trails. When planning your hike, it's recommended to check the specific route details on komoot for information regarding parking availability at the trailhead.
tips: The circular itinerary of the Lazarillo trail, marked out PR-TO 14, is a very picturesque route on the outskirts of the town of Almorox, crossing forests and meadows in a beautiful landscape setting. Along the route you will also find some sites of archaeological interest such as the anthropomorphic tomb and the inscription of San Julián.

It is a granite stone almost 7 meters high in which you can see a carving representing two people in front of a third. Studies on it have been able to decipher a writing in its lower part that alludes to the Roman goddess Diana and from there it is believed that it forms part of a series of elements found in the area that represented the border between the Roman provinces of Hispania Citerior. and Lusitania.

faqs.question: Where does the Sendero del Lazarillo trail start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The Sendero del Lazarillo (PR-TO 14) is a circular route that begins and ends in Almorox, Toledo. While specific parking areas aren't detailed, Almorox is the main starting point, and you can typically find parking within the town or near the trailhead. Look for designated parking spots or areas suitable for hikers.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this hike?
faqs.answer: This trail is generally considered to be of moderate difficulty. It involves some ascents and descents, covering a distance of approximately 16 kilometers with an elevation gain of around 300 meters. It's suitable for most hikers with a reasonable level of fitness.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Sendero del Lazarillo, and are there any specific rules?
faqs.answer: Yes, dogs are generally allowed on the Sendero del Lazarillo. However, it's always best to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through areas where livestock (like sheep herds and horses) might be present, or near wildlife. Please remember to clean up after your pet to help keep the trail enjoyable for everyone.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike the Sendero del Lazarillo is typically during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) months. During these seasons, the weather is usually milder and more pleasant for hiking, and the natural scenery is at its most vibrant. Summers can be very hot, and winters might bring colder temperatures, though the trail is generally accessible year-round.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay any entrance fees to hike the Sendero del Lazarillo?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need a permit, nor are there any entrance fees required to hike the Sendero del Lazarillo (PR-TO 14). It's a publicly accessible trail.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: You'll experience diverse terrain on this route. It traverses extensive pine forests, particularly the impressive Pinar de Almorox, and follows sections of the ancient Cañada Real de Talavera. The path also leads through river basins and features changing elevations, offering a varied and engaging hiking experience.

faqs.question: What notable features or points of interest will I see along the way?
faqs.answer: Beyond the natural beauty, the trail is rich in points of interest. You'll hike through the Sendero del Lazarillo itself, which is inspired by the classic novel *Lazarillo de Tormes*. A key highlight is the area around the "written rock," which refers to the Peña Escrita de Cenicientos (a prominent rock formation with views) and the nearby Piedra Escrita, an archaeological site with ancient engravings. You'll also encounter natural springs like Fuente de la Adrada, and pass near the archaeological site of Ermita de San Julián.

faqs.question: Is the Sendero del Lazarillo suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: Given its moderate difficulty and approximately 16-kilometer length, the trail might be challenging for very young children. However, older children who are accustomed to hiking and have good stamina should be able to enjoy it. The diverse scenery and historical elements can make it an engaging experience for families.

faqs.question: What wildlife might I encounter on this hike?
faqs.answer: The trail passes through the Pinar de Almorox, which is home to various wildlife. You might spot squirrels, deer, and wild boars. Additionally, as parts of the route follow the Cañada Real de Talavera, it's common to encounter traditional rural life, including sheep herds and horses.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other hiking paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Sendero del Lazarillo (PR-TO 14) intersects with and follows sections of several other paths. Notably, it covers a significant portion of the Sendero del Lazarillo and also includes parts of the Camino de los Enebrales and the Cañada de Talavera. You might also briefly encounter sections of the Ruta del Camino de Santiago desde Levante.

faqs.question: Are there any natural water sources along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, there are several natural springs along the way, including the Fuente de la Adrada. While these can be a source of water, it's always recommended to carry sufficient water for your hike, especially during warmer months, and to treat any natural water before drinking.
